jsonLdWeb.name: Aquarium Volume Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate aquarium volume in gallons and liters for multiple tank shapes with equipment sizing recommendations.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate aquarium volume?

acceptedAnswer.text: For rectangular tanks: Length × Width × Height (all in inches) ÷ 231 = US gallons. For cylindrical tanks: π × radius² × height ÷ 231. Always measure inside dimensions for accuracy.

mainEntity.name: How much does aquarium water weigh?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fresh water weighs 8.34 lbs per gallon. A 55-gallon tank holds about 459 lbs of water alone. Salt water weighs about 8.56 lbs per gallon. Always ensure your floor and stand can support the total weight.

mainEntity.name: How many watts per gallon for a heater?

acceptedAnswer.text: Use 3-5 watts per gallon as a guideline. For rooms that stay warm (75°F+), 3 watts/gallon is fine. For cooler rooms or larger temperature differences, use 5 watts/gallon. Larger tanks over 75 gallons benefit from two smaller heaters.

mainEntity.name: How much substrate do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: For a 2-inch substrate bed: multiply tank length × width (in inches), divide by 10, and that gives approximate pounds of gravel needed. For planted tanks, aim for 3-4 inches of substrate.

mainEntity.name: What size tank do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: General rule: 1 inch of fish per gallon for small community fish. However, larger or messier fish need much more space. A 20-gallon is the minimum recommended starter tank for stability.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate bow-front tank volume?

acceptedAnswer.text: A bow-front is roughly a rectangle plus a partial cylinder. The formula approximates: (Length × Width × Height) + (bow depth × Length × Height × 0.5) for the curved section, all divided by 231 for gallons.

howItWorks:
How the Aquarium Volume Calculator Works

Volume is calculated based on tank shape using inside dimensions, then converted to gallons and liters.

Rectangle: L × W × H ÷ 231 = US Gallons
  • Rectangle: L × W × H (cubic inches) ÷ 231
  • Cylinder: π × r² × H ÷ 231
  • Bow-front: Rectangle volume + bow section volume
  • Hexagonal: (3√3 / 2) × s² × H ÷ 231
  • Water weight: Gallons × 8.34 lbs
  • Heater: 3-5 watts per gallon depending on room temperature
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How do I calculate aquarium volume?

For rectangular tanks, multiply Length × Width × Height (in inches) and divide by 231 to get US gallons. Use inside dimensions for accuracy, as glass thickness reduces actual water volume.

How much does aquarium water weigh?

Fresh water weighs 8.34 lbs per gallon. A filled 55-gallon tank holds about 459 lbs of water plus the weight of substrate, decorations, and the tank itself (often 70-100+ lbs total added).

How many watts per gallon for a heater?

Use 3-5 watts per gallon. In warm rooms, 3 watts/gallon suffices. In cooler rooms or for a bigger temperature lift, go to 5 watts/gallon. Tanks over 75 gallons do best with two smaller heaters for even heat.

How much substrate do I need?

For a 2-inch gravel bed, multiply length × width in inches and divide by 10 to get approximate pounds needed. Planted tanks typically need 3-4 inches of substrate, so increase accordingly.

What size tank do I need?

The old rule is 1 inch of fish per gallon, but this only applies to small, slim community fish. Larger fish, messy fish, and territorial species need far more. A 20-gallon is the minimum for beginners.

How do I calculate bow-front tank volume?

A bow-front adds a curved section to the front of a rectangular base. Approximate it as the rectangular volume plus about 50% of the bow depth × length × height, then divide by 231 for gallons.
